title: olearia pannosa subsp. pannosa
intro: Olearia pannosa subsp. pannosa, also known as New Zealand flax-leaved daisy, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its bioactive compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Olearia pannosa subsp. pannosa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
